title: ED safeguarding documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.

source.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook
source.jurisdiction: International guidance requiring UAE legal, safeguarding and referral adaptation
source.population: Women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence; the handbook does not directly address people under 18 or men.
source.setting: Health-care encounters including first-line support, history, examination and documentation.
source.exclusions: Exact for survivor-centred first-line support, consent to documentation, recording complaints, symptoms, signs and injuries, and history or examination only when clinically performed. It is not authority for universal screening or automatic reporting in the UAE.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.

assessment.text: Supports child-centred, private and accessible communication, consent to examination, and recording actual words and disagreements.
assessment.rationale: For children and young people, the exact NICE section supports recording patient concerns, consent-based assessment and completed risk or consent discussion in the child’s actual words where appropriate.

source.title: Child abuse and neglect
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; UAE safeguarding law and pathways govern local use
source.population: Children and young people under 18, including unborn babies, who may be at risk of or experiencing abuse or neglect, and their parents or carers.
source.setting: Health, emergency, social-care and other services encountering children and families.
source.exclusions: Exact for child-centred communication, consent to examination, recording actual words, non-leading exploration, observing the child and relationships, and clinician-led safeguarding action. It does not assert abuse or replace UAE reporting requirements.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports child-centred, private and accessible communication, consent to examination, and recording actual words and disagreements.

assessment.text: Supports recognising indirect disclosure, using open non-leading questions, explaining next steps and recording local safeguarding referral when clinician-completed.
assessment.rationale: For children and young people, the exact recognition section supports associated context, relevant negatives, clinician risk context, safeguarding concern and clinician-completed action without asserting abuse.

source.title: Child abuse and neglect
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; UAE safeguarding law and pathways govern local use
source.population: Children and young people under 18, including unborn babies, who may be at risk of or experiencing abuse or neglect, and their parents or carers.
source.setting: Health, emergency, social-care and other services encountering children and families.
source.exclusions: Exact for child-centred communication, consent to examination, recording actual words, non-leading exploration, observing the child and relationships, and clinician-led safeguarding action. It does not assert abuse or replace UAE reporting requirements.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recognising indirect disclosure, using open non-leading questions, explaining next steps and recording local safeguarding referral when clinician-completed.

assessment.text: Supports observing the child and interactions, communicating directly and privately where appropriate, non-leading exploration and structured family or care-environment context.
assessment.rationale: The exact child-assessment section supports focused assessment, appearance, clinician plan, specialist discussion and arranged handover only when performed.

assessment.text: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.
assessment.rationale: For adult women experiencing intimate-partner violence, the exact WHO documentation section supports consent-based recording of concerns, symptoms, signs and injuries; it does not cover all adult safeguarding groups.

curation_policy: Direct full-source guideline reconstruction; every legacy item has an auditable comparison and every final clinical item retains exact source provenance.
